LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN CHIANG'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS CONTINUED FROM APR-16-2009 TO LAW AND MOTION AT MAY-04-2009, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED TO MAY 4, 2009. PLAINTIFF NEEDS TO FILE HER OPPOSITION WITH THE COURT. JUDGE: QUENTIN L. KOPP, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN CHIANG, CONTROLLER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS GRANTED. THE ISSUE OF WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS ENTITLED TO THE SUBJECT ESCHEATED FUNDS WAS PREVIOUSLY ADJUDICATED BY THE ALAMEDA SUPERIOR COURT IN CASE NUMBER RP03-131 453. PLAINTIFF IS BARRED FROM RELITIGATING THAT ISSUE IN THIS ACTION. NO OTHER ISSUE IS BEFORE THE COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar May-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOHN CHIANG, CONTROLLER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted. The issue of whether plaintiff was entitled to the subject escheated funds was previously adjudicated by the Alameda Superior Court in case number RP03-131 453. Plaintiff is barred from relitigating that issue in this action. No other issue is before the Court. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
